reason: Canvas runtime context spans frontend, API, runtime session and local/backend execution boundaries.
Canvas runtime snapshot and bridge DTO changes must remain generated contract facts.
Runtime resource surface and VFS surface refs are the resource access contract.
Canvas runtime action and backend placement must follow RuntimeGateway admission boundaries.
Existing CanvasAgentRunContext is the binding layer that implementation must reuse and deepen.
Existing AgentRun Canvas runtime observation/interaction latest state defines binding ownership fields.
Migration documents that RuntimeSession is a derived trace field, not the Canvas runtime state ownership key.
Workspace Module command structs and Canvas operations are the local interface to converge around a shared runtime context.
Canvas runtime surface updates currently assemble runtime session, bridge, VFS and user facts that should flow from the shared Workspace Module runtime context.
Workspace Module tool providers construct operate/invoke/present commands and must pass the converged runtime context instead of parallel runtime fact sets.
Runtime tool provider builds Workspace Module runtime inputs from ExecutionContext and is the adapter into the new local context.
AgentRun current runtime surface query is the source for delivery runtime VFS/backend/resource surface projection.
